zh.lifeSummary: 當Joseph Ecem Hughes於1890年1月24日出生在美國阿肯色州霍特 斯普林俾斯麥時，他的父親William Marion Hughes33歲，母親Amy Jane Diffee40歲。他和Mattie Eleanor Massey至少有3個兒子和5個女兒。他於1935年住在美國阿肯色州克萊且於1940年住在美國加州柯恩朱迪希阿爾 堂史普 1。他去世於1957年5月20日在美國加州柯恩貝可斯費爾德享年67歲，埋葬在美國加州柯恩貝可斯費爾德格林拉 梅莫利亞爾 帕克。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Joseph Ecem Hughes于1890年1月24日出生在美国阿肯色州霍特 斯普林俾斯麥时，他的父亲William Marion Hughes33岁，母亲Amy Jane Diffee40岁。他和Mattie Eleanor Massey至少有3个儿子和5个女儿。他于1935年住在美国阿肯色州克萊且于1940年住在美国加州柯恩朱迪希阿爾 堂史普 1。他去世于1957年5月20日在美国加州柯恩貝可斯費爾德享年67岁，埋葬在美国加州柯恩貝可斯費爾德格林拉 梅莫利亞爾 帕克。

nameMeanings.name: Hughes
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English and Welsh: variant of Hugh with genitival or excrescent -s.
Irish and Scottish: adopted as an equivalent of Gaelic surnames based on the personal name Aodh ‘fire’, for example Ó hAodha, Mac Aodha; see McCoy and compare McHugh .

variants.name: Hugh
meanings: English, Welsh, and Scottish (of Norman origin): from the Old French personal name Hue, from ancient Germanic Hugo (ultimately derived from hugi- ‘mind, spirit’). This was a very popular name in England after the Norman Conquest, partly due to the reputation of Saint Hugh of Lincoln (1140–1200), who was born in Burgundy and who established the first Carthusian monastery in England.
Scottish and Irish: adopted as an Anglicized form of the Gaelic personal name Aodh, a derivative of a Gaelic word meaning ‘fire’. See Hughes .
Chinese: variant Romanization of the surnames 邱 and 丘, based on their Hakka pronunciation; see   Qiu   1 and 2.

nameMeanings.name: Joseph
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: form of the biblical Hebrew name Yosef, meaning ‘(God) shall add (another son)’. This was borne by the favourite son of Jacob, whose brothers became jealous of him and sold him into slavery (Genesis 37). He was taken to Egypt, where he rose to become chief steward to Pharaoh, and was eventually reconciled to his brothers when they came to buy corn during a seven-year famine (Genesis 43–7). In the New Testament Joseph is the name of the husband of the Virgin Mary. It is also borne by a rich Jew, Joseph of Arimathea (Matthew 27:57; Mark 15:43; Luke 23:50; John 19:38), who took Jesus down from the Cross, wrapped him in a shroud, and buried him in a rock tomb. According to medieval legend, Joseph of Arimathea brought the Holy Grail to Britain. The name was uncommon in Britain in the Middle Ages but was revived in the mid 16th century and had become popular by the 1630s, remaining so ever since.

variants.name: Josef
meanings: , Dutch, Scandinavian, and Czech spelling of   Joseph  , now also in use in the English‐speaking world.

nameMeanings.name: Ecem
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.

historicEvents.title: Angel Island Serves as Quarantine Station
historicEvents.description: Angel Island served as a quarantine station for those diagnosed with bubonic plague beginning in 1891. A quarantine station was built on the island which was funded by the federal government at the cost of $98,000. The disease spread to port cities around the world, including the San Francisco Bay Area, during the third bubonic plague pandemic, which lasted through 1909.
historicEvents.urlTitle: California Department of Parks and Recreation - Angel Island

historicEvents.title: Plessy vs. Ferguson
historicEvents.description: A landmark decision of the U.S. Supreme Court upholding the constitutionality of racial segregation laws for public facilities if the segregated facilities were equal in quality. It's widely regarded as one of the worst decisions in U.S. Supreme Court history.

historicEvents.title: The Bureau of Investigation is formed
historicEvents.description: Known as the National Bureau of Criminal Identification, The Bureau of Investigation helped agencies across the country identify different criminals. President Roosevelt instructed that there be an autonomous investigative service that would report only to the Attorney General.

historicEvents.title: Angel Island Serves Immigrants
historicEvents.description: Angel Island served as a western entry point for hundreds of thousands of U.S. immigrants, mainly from China, from 1910 to 1940.
Charles Lindbergh makes the first solo nonstop transatlantic flight in his plane The Spirit of St. Louis.

historicEvents.title: Alcatraz Island Becomes Federal Penitentiary
historicEvents.description: Alcatraz Island officially became Alcatraz Federal Penitentiary on August 11, 1934. The island is situated in the middle of frigid water and strong currents of the San Francisco Bay, which deemed it virtually inescapable. Alcatraz became known as the toughest prison in America and was seen as a “last resort prison.” Therefore, Alcatraz housed some of America’s most notorious prisoners such as Al Capone and Robert Franklin Stroud. Due to the exorbitant cost of running the prison, and the deterioration of the buildings due to salt spray, Alcatraz Island closed as a penitentiary on March 21, 1963.

historicEvents.title: The Japanese American internment
historicEvents.description: Caused by the tensions between the United States and the Empire of Japan, the internment of Japanese Americans caused many to be forced out of their homes and forcibly relocated into concentration camps in the western states. More than 110,000 Japanese Americans were forced into these camps in fear that some of them were spies for Japan.

historicEvents.title: Grizzly Bear Becomes State Animal
historicEvents.description: The California grizzly bear became designated as the state animal in 1953.

en.lifeSummary: When Joseph Ecem Hughes was born on 24 January 1890, in Bismarck, Hot Spring, Arkansas, United States, his father, William Marion Hughes, was 33 and his mother, Amy Jane Diffee, was 40. He had at least 3 sons and 5 daughters with Mattie Eleanor Massey. He lived in Clay, Arkansas, United States in 1935 and Judicial Township 15, Kern, California, United States in 1940. He died on 20 May 1957, in Bakersfield, Kern, California, United States, at the age of 67, and was buried in Greenlawn Memorial Park, Bakersfield, Kern, California, United States.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Joseph Ecem Hughes est né le 24 janvier 1890, à Bismarck, Hot Spring, Arkansas, États-Unis, son père, William Marion Hughes, avait 33 ans et sa mère, Amy Jane Diffee, avait 40 ans. Il a eu au moins 3 fils et 5 filles avec Mattie Eleanor Massey. Il a vécu à Clay, Arkansas, États-Unis en 1935 et à Judicial Township 15, Kern, Californie, États-Unis en 1940. Il est décédé le 20 mai 1957, à Bakersfield, Kern, Californie, États-Unis, à l’âge de 67 ans, et a été enterré à Greenlawn Memorial Park, Bakersfield, Kern, Californie, États-Unis.

de.lifeSummary: Als Joseph Ecem Hughes am 24. Januar 1890 in Bismarck, Hot Spring, Arkansas, Vereinigte Staaten geboren wurde, war sein Vater William Marion Hughes 33 und seine Mutter Amy Jane Diffee 40. it.lifeSummary: Quando Joseph Ecem Hughes è nato il il 24 gennaio 1890 nel Bismarck, Hot Spring, Arkansas, Stati Uniti d'America, il suo padre, William Marion Hughes, aveva 33 anni e il suo madre, Amy Jane Diffee, aveva 40 anni. Lui avuto almeno 3 figli e 5 figlie con Mattie Eleanor Massey. Vissuto a Clay, Arkansas, Stati Uniti d'America nel 1935 e nel Judicial Township 15, Kern, California, Stati Uniti d'America nel 1940. Morì il 20 maggio 1957, nel Bakersfield, Kern, California, Stati Uniti d'America, all'età di 67 anni, e fu sepolto a Greenlawn Memorial Park, Bakersfield, Kern, California, Stati Uniti d'America.

ja.lifeSummary: Joseph Ecem Hughesが1890年1月24日にアメリカ合衆国アーカンソー州ホート スプリングビスマルックで生まれた時，父William Marion Hughesは33歳，母Amy Jane Diffeeは40歳であった。彼はMattie Eleanor Masseyとの間に少なくとも3人の息子と5人の娘をもうけた。彼は1935年にはアメリカ合衆国アーカンソー州クレイに，1940年にはアメリカ合衆国カリフォルニア州ケルンジュディシアル トウンシュイップ 1に住んでいた。彼は1957年5月20日にアメリカ合衆国カリフォルニア州ケルンベイケルスフィールドで67歳で死去し，アメリカ合衆国カリフォルニア州ケルンベイケルスフィールドグレエンラウン メモーイアル パークにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Joseph Ecem Hughes(이)가 1890년 1월 24일 미국 아칸소 주 호트 스플링 비스마크에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 William Marion Hughes(은)는 33세였고, 그의 어머니 Amy Jane Diffee(은)는 40세였다. 그는 Mattie Eleanor Massey(와)과 적어도 세 명의 아들과 다섯 명의 딸을 두었다. 1935년도에 미국 아칸소 주 클레이에서, 1940년도에 미국 캘리포니아 주 커 주딕이앨 타운시프 1에서 살았다. 그는 1957년 5월 20일 미국 캘리포니아 주 커 베이커스필드에서 67세의 나이로 사망했으며 미국 캘리포니아 주 커 베이커스필드 그린로 모리앨 박에 묻혔다.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Joseph Ecem Hughes родился 24 января 1890 г. в местности бисмаркк, хот спринг, Арканзас, Соединенные штаты, его отцу по имени William Marion Hughes было 33 года, а матери, которую звали Amy Jane Diffee, 40. У него было по крайней мере 3 сына и 5 дочерей от Mattie Eleanor Massey. Он жил в местности клеи, Арканзас, Соединенные штаты в 1935 году и в местности джудисяль тоуншип 1, кирн, Калифорния, Соединенные штаты в 1940 году. Он умер 20 мая 1957 г. в местности беикирсфиелд, кирн, Калифорния, Соединенные штаты в возрасте 67 лет и был похоронен в местности гринлавн меморяль парк, беикирсфиелд, кирн, Калифорния, Соединенные штаты.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Joseph Ecem Hughes nació el 24 de enero de 1890, en Bismarck, Hot Spring, Arkansas, Estados Unidos, su papá, William Marion Hughes, tenía 33 años y su mamá, Amy Jane Diffee, tenía 40 años. Tuvo por lo menos 3 hijos y 5 hijas con Mattie Eleanor Massey. Vivió en Clay, Arkansas, Estados Unidos en 1935 y en Judicial Township 15, Kern, California, Estados Unidos en 1940. Murió el 20 de mayo de 1957, en Bakersfield, Kern, California, Estados Unidos, a la edad de 67 años, y fue sepultado en Greenlawn Memorial Park, Bakersfield, Kern, California, Estados Unidos.
